batches.title: Boiled Eggs (bulk, whole week)
steps: Bring large pot of water to rolling boil
Gently lower 14 large eggs into boiling water
Boil 9 minutes for soft-set yolk, 12 minutes for hard-set
Immediately transfer to ice bath with ice water to stop cooking
Let rest in ice bath 5 minutes until completely cooled
Peel by tapping all over and rolling gently to crack shell, peel under cool running water
Pat dry with paper towels, store in airtight container in fridge

day_of_assembly.Monday: Dinner — Boiled Egg & Cheese Plate with Whole-Grain Pita & Avocado: halve 2 boiled eggs, arrange on plate with gruyère, pita, avocado and baby spinach
day_of_assembly.Tuesday: Breakfast — Herb-Crusted Boiled Egg with Sourdough & Blueberries: halve 1 boiled egg, plate on toasted sourdough with dill and blueberries
day_of_assembly.Wednesday: Breakfast — Herb-Crusted Boiled Egg with Sourdough & Peach: halve 2 boiled eggs, plate on toasted sourdough with thyme and fresh peach slices
day_of_assembly.Thursday: Breakfast — Herb-Crusted Boiled Egg with Sourdough & Strawberries: halve 2 boiled eggs, plate on toasted sourdough with basil and strawberries
day_of_assembly.Friday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (eggs cooked is only safe ~4 day(s); this is day 5).
day_of_assembly.Saturday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (eggs cooked is only safe ~4 day(s); this is day 6).
day_of_assembly.Sunday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (eggs cooked is only safe ~4 day(s); this is day 7).

storage.safety_note: Hard-boiled eggs: 1 week in shell, 5 days peeled in water. Scrambled/cooked dishes: 3–4 days.

batches.title: Mediterranean Grilled Chicken & Quinoa Base
steps: Pat 800g chicken breasts dry with paper towels
Combine 60ml olive oil, juice of 2 lemons, 4 minced garlic cloves, 2 tbsp fresh oregano, salt and pepper in bowl
Place chicken in zip-lock bag or large dish, pour marinade over, coat well
Marinate 2–4 hours in fridge (or overnight for deeper flavour)
Preheat grill or grill pan to medium-high heat
Grill marinated chicken 6–7 minutes per side until 165°C internal temperature, turning once
Rest grilled chicken 5 minutes, cool to room temperature
Cut 300g dry quinoa and 600g mixed vegetables into prep portions
Roast 600g mixed raw vegetables (diced red peppers, zucchini, halved cherry tomatoes) at 200°C for 25 minutes until tender and lightly caramelised
Cool roasted vegetables completely
Cook 300g dry quinoa per package directions, drain, cool

day_of_assembly.Monday: Lunch — Mediterranean Grilled Chicken with Roasted Vegetables & Quinoa: plate 180g grilled chicken, 150g roasted veg (red pepper 80g, zucchini 50g, tomato 20g), 100g quinoa, drizzle with 2 tsp olive oil and lemon juice
day_of_assembly.Saturday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (poultry is only safe ~3 day(s); this is day 6).

storage.safety_note: Cool quickly; never leave poultry at room temp > 2 h. Reheat to 74 °C through.

batches.title: Salmon Fillet & Roasted Vegetables (Part 1: Vegetables)
steps: Trim 400g fresh asparagus spears, discard tough bottom 2 inches
Bring pot of salted water to boil, add asparagus, blanch 2 minutes until bright green
Drain asparagus, plunge into ice water to stop cooking, pat completely dry with paper towels
Toss blanched asparagus with 2 tsp olive oil, 2 minced garlic cloves, salt and pepper
Spread on baking sheet in single layer
Peel 500g sweet potatoes, cut into uniform 1cm thick wedges
Toss sweet potato wedges with 1 tbsp olive oil, salt and pepper
Spread on separate baking sheet
Roast both trays at 200°C for 12 minutes (asparagus) and 20 minutes (sweet potato) until tender
Cool both completely, store separately

day_of_assembly.Tuesday: Lunch — Pan-Seared Salmon with Roasted Asparagus & Sweet Potato: pan-sear fresh 160g salmon fillet (raw, not from prep), plate with 120g roasted asparagus and 140g roasted sweet potato, drizzle with 1.5 tsp olive oil and lemon juice, garnish with fresh dill
day_of_assembly.Saturday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (vegetables cooked is only safe ~4 day(s); this is day 6).

storage.safety_note: Cooked vegetables store well. Roasted veg can be eaten cold in salads.

batches.title: Mexican-Style Seasoned Shredded Chicken & Black Bean Mixture
steps: Use 400g reserved cooked chicken from batch 2 (Mediterranean grilled chicken)
Shred cooked chicken into bite-sized pieces using two forks
Combine shredded chicken with 2 tsp ground cumin, 1 tsp paprika, 2 minced garlic cloves, juice of 1 lime, salt and pepper, toss well
Cover and refrigerate until assembly
Drain and rinse 1 × 400g can black beans (about 250g drained weight)
Heat 1 tsp olive oil in pot over medium heat
Add 1 minced garlic clove and drained black beans, stir
Season with 1 tsp ground cumin, salt and pepper
Warm through 2 minutes, then remove from heat
Squeeze juice of 1 lime over beans, stir gently
Cool completely, refrigerate in separate container

day_of_assembly.Tuesday: Dinner — Mexican-Style Chicken Lettuce Wrap with Black Beans & Lime: reheat 130g seasoned chicken and 70g black bean mixture, serve in 3 large romaine lettuce leaves with 40g diced red pepper and fresh cilantro, serve lime wedge on side

storage.safety_note: Cool quickly; never leave poultry at room temp > 2 h. Reheat to 74 °C through.

batches.title: Spiced Turkey Meatballs, Brown Lentils & Roasted Beets
steps: Combine 500g ground turkey, 30g whole-grain breadcrumbs, 1 large egg, 2 tsp ground cumin, 1 tsp paprika, 3 minced garlic cloves, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.25 tsp black pepper in large bowl
Mix gently with hands until just combined (do not overwork)
Shape mixture into 16 equal meatballs (about 30g each)
Arrange on parchment-lined baking sheet
Roast at 190°C for 18 minutes until internal temperature reaches 74°C
Cool meatballs on tray, store in container
Rinse 250g dried brown lentils, discard debris
Bring 600ml water to boil in pot, add rinsed lentils
Reduce heat, simmer 20–25 minutes until tender but still holding shape
Drain lentils, cool completely
Peel 300g fresh beets, cut into 1cm cubes
Toss beet cubes with 1 tbsp olive oil, 1 minced garlic clove, salt and pepper
Roast on baking sheet at 200°C for 20 minutes until caramelised
Cool roasted beets completely

day_of_assembly.Wednesday: Lunch — Spiced Turkey Meatballs with Brown Lentil Salad & Roasted Beets: reheat 3 turkey meatballs, plate on warm tossed salad of 60g cooked lentils, 50g roasted beets, 30g rocket leaves, 1 tsp olive oil and lemon juice

storage.safety_note: Cool quickly; never leave poultry at room temp > 2 h. Reheat to 74 °C through.

batches.title: Grilled Beef Steak & Chickpea Purée with Roasted Bell Peppers
steps: Combine 45ml olive oil, 4 minced garlic cloves, 1 tbsp fresh rosemary leaves, 1 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp black pepper in bowl
Pat 500g sirloin steak dry, place in zip-lock bag or dish
Pour marinade over steak, coat both sides, seal or cover
Marinate 30 minutes to 2 hours in fridge
Cut 400g mixed red and yellow bell peppers into large chunks, discard seeds
Toss peppers with 1 tbsp olive oil, salt and pepper
Roast on baking sheet at 200°C for 20 minutes until soft and caramelised
Cool roasted peppers, store separately
Drain 2 × 225g cans chickpeas (about 500g total drained weight), reserve 250g for this batch
Combine 250g drained chickpeas, 2 tbsp tahini, juice of 1 lemon, 2 minced garlic cloves, 3 tbsp water, salt and pepper in food processor
Blend until completely smooth, adding more water if needed for creamy consistency
Transfer hummus to container, drizzle with 1 tsp olive oil, cover

day_of_assembly.Thursday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (red meat is only safe ~3 day(s); this is day 4).

storage.safety_note: Store in coldest part of fridge. Reheat to 74 °C before serving.

batches.title: Chicken & Chickpea Curry with Spinach
steps: Cut 400g raw chicken breast into 2cm dice
Peel and dice 200g onion (cooked gently, not raw, for nutrition plan compliance)
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in large pot over medium-high heat
Add diced onion, sauté 3 minutes until beginning to soften
Add diced chicken, 3 minced garlic cloves, 2 tsp curry powder, 1 tsp ground turmeric, 1 tsp grated fresh ginger
Stir and cook 6 minutes until chicken is opaque and cooked through
Drain and rinse 1 × 400g can chickpeas (about 250g drained), add to pot
Add 1 × 400g can chopped tomatoes, 100ml vegetable stock, stir well
Reduce heat to medium-low, simmer 12 minutes until flavours meld
Tear 150g fresh spinach, add to pot, stir
Cook 2 minutes until spinach is completely wilted
Taste and adjust seasoning with salt, pepper and lime juice
Cool to room temperature, transfer to storage container, refrigerate
yields: 800g total curry (chicken, chickpeas, spinach sauce — divided into 4 × 200g portions)

day_of_assembly.Friday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (soup stew is only safe ~4 day(s); this is day 5).

storage.safety_note: Bring to a rolling boil when reheating. Excellent for freezing in individual portions.

batches.title: Herb-Roasted Chicken, Root Vegetables & Quinoa
steps: Combine 60ml olive oil, 5 minced garlic cloves, 2 tbsp fresh rosemary, 1 tbsp fresh thyme, 1 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp black pepper in bowl
Pat 700g chicken breasts dry with paper towels
Place in large zip-lock bag or dish, pour herb marinade over, coat thoroughly
Marinate 2–4 hours or overnight in fridge for deeper flavour
Preheat oven to 190°C
Arrange marinated chicken on baking sheet, roast 22–25 minutes until internal temperature reaches 165°C
Cool chicken completely on tray
Peel 500g mixed root vegetables (carrots, parsnips), cut into uniform batons and wedges
Toss vegetables with 1.5 tbsp olive oil, salt and pepper
Spread on baking sheet, roast at 200°C for 25 minutes until tender and caramelised
Cool roasted vegetables completely
Cook 250g dried quinoa per package directions (typically 1 part quinoa to 2 parts water, simmer 15 min)
Drain quinoa if needed, cool completely
yields: 700g roasted herb chicken (reserve 400g for batch 4 shredded use, use remainder as intact fillets)

day_of_assembly.Sunday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (poultry is only safe ~3 day(s); this is day 7).

storage.safety_note: Cool quickly; never leave poultry at room temp > 2 h. Reheat to 74 °C through.

batches.title: Roasted Mixed Vegetables, Cooked Brown Lentils & Tahini Dressing
steps: Cut 500g mixed vegetables (broccoli florets, red bell pepper chunks, carrot batons) into similar-sized pieces
Toss with 2 tbsp olive oil, 2 minced garlic cloves, salt and pepper
Spread on baking sheet in single layer
Roast at 200°C for 20 minutes until tender and lightly caramelised
Cool roasted vegetables completely
Rinse 300g dried brown lentils, pick through for debris
Bring 750ml water to boil, add rinsed lentils
Reduce heat to medium-low, simmer 20–25 minutes until tender
Drain lentils completely, cool to room temperature
Combine 3 tbsp tahini, juice of 2 lemons, 1 minced garlic clove, 1/4 tsp salt, pinch of black pepper in bowl
Whisk in 2–3 tbsp warm water gradually until pourable dressing consistency achieved
Transfer dressing to jar, refrigerate
yields: 150g tahini dressing (makes ~6 servings at 2–3 tbsp per bowl)

day_of_assembly.Sunday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (legumes is only safe ~5 day(s); this is day 7).

storage.safety_note: Cooked beans and lentils keep well. Freeze in portions for quick meals.
